summary: GH-96 Desktop Task Thread checkpoint is approved for merge as a docs-only product/design checkpoint. It correctly clarifies that App automatic task execution is limited to deterministic Lode workflows while App remains a global observability and management surface for non-App caller facts, Library, Browser, and manual controlled-browser launch.
reviewed_validation_summary: `git diff --check` pass；`jq empty .loom/specs/GH-96/build-evidence.json` pass；`loom suite validate --target . --item GH-96 --json` returned `not_applicable` with no missing inputs；`loom fact-chain --target . --json` pass。
follow_ups: Do not interpret Task Thread first as removing Library or Browser management surfaces.
Do not interpret the automatic task entry limitation as a WebEnvoy platform-wide limitation on Agent/API/CLI/MCP/SDK/skills callers.
Do not treat the versioned design image as a high-fidelity prototype, pixel spec, data contract, schema/API guarantee, runtime proof, or evidence policy.
#93/#94/#95 must still align real fields and states with Core/Harbor/Lode contracts before implementation.

semantic_review_disposition.reason: The docs consistently define App as a human desktop entry with Task Thread first UX, deterministic Lode workflow automatic execution, global observability for non-App caller facts, manual controlled-browser launch when no site skill exists, and preserved Library/Browser management surfaces. The PR remains docs-only and does not claim implementation completion.
